metadata.name: CDE Workflow
metadata.description: Information container states and transitions within the Common Data Environment according to ISO 19650 information management principles.
metadata.author: ISO19650 Knowledge Maps

nodes.label: Information Container
nodes.description: Named persistent set of information retrievable from within a file, system or application storage hierarchy.

nodes.label: WORK IN PROGRESS
nodes.description: State where information is being developed by its authoring appointed party and is not visible or accessible to other parties. This is the only state where information can be generated or amended.

nodes.label: SHARED
nodes.description: State where information is shared with other parties within the information production team for coordination, review, and collaborative development.

nodes.label: PUBLISHED
nodes.description: State where information has been authorized by the lead appointed party and accepted by the appointing party for use. It is the most current revision.

nodes.label: ARCHIVED
nodes.description: State where superseded information containers are retained for audit, historical reference, or legal purposes, but are no longer approved for active use.

nodes.label: Check / Review / Approve
nodes.description: Process where the appointed party verifies the information container against the information production standard and approves it for a specific use before sharing.

nodes.label: Review / Authorize
nodes.description: Process where the lead appointed party reviews the information model against information production requirements and authorizes it for submission to the appointing party.

nodes.label: Review / Accept
nodes.description: Process where the appointing party reviews the submitted information model against the information production requirements and accepts it for use.

nodes.label: Status Code
nodes.description: Property signifying the permitted use of an information container's content, determined by which workflow state it is in and the use for which it has been approved.

nodes.label: Common Data Environment
nodes.description: Operational framework combining the information production workflow and enabling technologies to facilitate collaborative information production.

nodes.label: Appointed Party
nodes.description: Party responsible for generating and coordinating information containers within an information production team.

nodes.label: Lead Appointed Party
nodes.description: Party responsible for managing information production, coordinating appointed parties, and authorizing information models before submission.

nodes.label: Appointing Party
nodes.description: Party accountable for initiating projects, defining information requirements, and accepting information models.

evidence.quote: Information containers start the information production workflow in the WORK IN PROGRESS state.
This is the only state where information within an information container can be generated or amended by its appointed party.
Information containers are checked, reviewed, and approved for a specific use before being shared with other parties.

When an information container is ready to be shared... it transitions from the WORK IN PROGRESS state to the SHARED state. Before this transition occurs, the information within it is checked, reviewed, and approved.
When an information container is ready to be shared... it transitions from the WORK IN PROGRESS state to the SHARED state.
Information containers may return to work in progress following coordination feedback.
Prior to submitting an information model to the appointing party... the lead appointed party shall undertake a review of each information container.
The lead appointed party reviews and authorizes information models in line with the information production workflow before they are submitted to the appointing party.
Information containers that have been accepted by the appointing party, transition to the PUBLISHED state.
authorize the information container(s)... and submit the information container(s) to the appointing party for review and acceptance
The appointing party shall undertake a review of the information model... If the review is successful: accept the information container(s).
If the review is successful: accept the information container(s)... publish the information container(s).
If the review is unsuccessful... reject the information model; and instruct the lead appointed party to have the relevant information container(s) amended and resubmit.
Information containers that are superseded transition to the ARCHIVED state.
In addition to a workflow state, each revision of an information container is assigned a status.
